coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: Easy 6–7 miles, Z2 (130–140 bpm), flat or rolling terrain. You're in week 21 of taper with a recovery score of 62/100—moderate, not high. Your last three runs averaged 133–142 bpm, which is solid work, but Thursday's role is to flush legs and stay fresh for Saturday's long run. Keep it conversational. No hills, no tempo, no intervals. This is a shakeout day that maintains aerobic capacity without further stress.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: You slept 6.18 hours last night—solid, but in taper you want 7–8 consistently to maximize adaptation and CNS recovery. Eat carbs at breakfast and lunch to top off glycogen; aim for 5–6g/kg bodyweight daily during taper to support the long run on Saturday. Today's easy run won't demand huge intra-run calories, but fuel breakfast well and hydrate steadily throughout the day. Poor sleep compounds fatigue; prioritize tonight's sleep over any "extra" training stimulus.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: Your 30-day sleep average is 4.83 hours—that's dangerously low for an ultra endurance athlete in peak training, even in taper. Last night's 6.18h is a step up, but you need to stabilize at 7–7.5h minimum through race week to consolidate VO2 max, VO2 kinetics, and injury resilience. Blackout room, no screens after 21:00, consistent bedtime. Resting HR at 60 bpm is solid; no signs of overtraining. Keep easy today to preserve sleep quality tonight.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: You're 92 days out from Julian Alps 50K with ~2,500m of vert—a serious alpine race that will break athletes who haven't trained specifically for sustained climbing. Taper anxiety is real, but the hard work is done. Use this week to mentally rehearse the course profile, test your nutrition strategy, and build confidence in your aerobic base. Your volume is dropping (19.1 mi this week vs 45 last week), which feels like "detraining"—it's not. You're sharpening. Trust the process; the mountains reward specificity and patience.
